audience: Engineers designing/onboarding to an async event-driven system

mode_descriptions.v1_monolith_compat: Transitional: order service still does sync DB write, then emits event. Other services react async.
mode_descriptions.v2_pure_events: Future state: order service emits event only; persistence happens in a downstream projector.

flows.name: Place Order
flows.purpose: Main user action. Differs significantly between v1 and v2 — show both.
step_outline_v2: order → kafka : produce order.created (NO direct DB write)
(async) kafka → orderdb : projector writes to read model

flows.name: Billing Reacts to Order
flows.purpose: Async fan-out: billing worker consumes the event and charges the card.
step_outline: kafka → billing : deliver order.created (consumer group: billing)
billing → kafka : produce billing.completed or billing.failed

flows.name: Notification Reacts to Order
flows.purpose: Parallel fan-out: notification worker emails the customer.

flows.name: Shipping Reacts (After Billing OK)
flows.purpose: Shipping waits for billing.completed before reserving inventory.

lessons: Fan-out is hard to show clearly. Each consumer becomes a separate flow rather than parallel arrows in one flow. The flow tab bar makes the parallelism obvious by listing them side-by-side.
The two modes here are MIGRATION states, not deployment environments. This is unusual but valid — modes can be any orthogonal axis as long as it changes payload shape, not topology.

When you have a mode that changes the SHAPE of a flow this much (6 steps vs 6 different steps), consider whether it should really be the same flow at all. Here it's worth keeping as one flow with mode-swapped payloads because the user story is identical from outside: 'place an order'.

Stripe is shown as a single named node (with a 'seed' role since it's external) only inside the billing_reaction flow. Don't pollute the main canvas with every external dependency — show each only in the flows where it's relevant. (Implementation tip: hide externals via mode or a third 'feature' toggle if needed.)
